summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes obfuscated Windows PE, then erases its own traces
details: The package advertises itself as an email confirmation utility, but its exported API (sendConfirmationEmail, verifyToken in index.js) is a stub that only console.logs and returns immediately — there is no real functionality. The actual behavior is delivered through scripts.postinstall, which runs install-hook.js. That script base64-decodes a ~380KB embedded blob, XORs every byte with 0x42 to reveal a Windows PE executable (MZ/PE header verified), writes it to %TEMP%/tmp_<timestamp>.exe, and spawns it detached via `cmd /c start /b` with windowsHide:true and stdio:'ignore'. After dropping the binary, the script writes a cleanup.js to %TEMP% and spawns it detached; cleanup.js waits 90 seconds, then removes `mailconfirmer` from the victim project's package.json dependencies/devDependencies, prunes it from package-lock.json, recursively deletes the module directory, and registers a Windows scheduled-task fallback (`schtasks /create... rmdir /s /q`) to ensure deletion. The dropped.exe continues running after the package erases itself from the install tree. This is a deliberate dropper with anti-forensics; on `npm install`, attacker-controlled native code is executed on the installer's Windows host with no user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
